symbolic_system.boundary: Rules governing claim ceilings, separation, non-laundering, cold review, and reflexive audit for RAE artifacts.

carrier.carrier_state: seed governance rules with typed claims, scoped validity, licensed consequences, and reflexive audit requirements
carrier.carrier_state_space: rule statements, separation rules, claim ceilings, non-laundering prohibitions, cold-review triggers, audit prompts
carrier.maintainer: RAE repo maintainers through changelog, program docs, and future review

use_episode.use: Use governance rules to decide whether a project artifact may be promoted, reused, or treated as licensed precedent.
use_episode.inference_drawn: An artifact with declared claim ceiling, scope, native warrant, correction, and non-laundering discipline may guide next work within that ceiling.
non_inferences: The artifact is true because it follows governance.
A reusable floor or intervention pattern may be promoted without cold review.

binding.binding_target: RAE artifact promotion and use discipline
binding.operative_mode: program rule surface for future work

validity.validity_criterion: Artifact use preserves declared claim ceiling, scope, warrant source, correction regime, and non-laundering rules.
validity.validity_evidence: The document explicitly states governance rules and cold review triggers.
validity.confidence: high for local governance intent; untested for repeated future use
validity.uncertainty: No cold-review implementation or automated enforcement exists yet.

scope.scope_conditions: RAE family artifacts and major promotion/use decisions.
prohibited_scope_extensions: Treating governance compliance as empirical truth.
Using governance text as production runtime.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing is granted by project-maintainer adoption and later cold-review use; not by the document alone.
recognition_authority.authority_source: RAE program repo and maintainer recognition.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: README/current-state references, changelog, workstream use, cold-review discipline.

policy_license.action_policy: Program governance non-laundering and cold-review rules.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Governance may require demotion, review, or blocked promotion within RAE scope.
policy_license.license_basis: Rule text plus recognizer standing plus project scope.

transmission.transmission_path: governance doc -> future project work -> artifact promotion decisions

verdict.next_check: Cold-review trigger needs executable or procedural support.

reflexive_pressure.notes: The artifact requires the full chain because its consequence depends on governance use by standing recognizers.

symbolic_system.boundary: Packet fields for actuality, possibility, reachability, floors, response ecology, symbolic carriers, interventions, licensed actions, remainder, and return capture.

carrier.carrier_state: structured YAML-like packet sections and questions
carrier.carrier_state_space: header fields, claim lists, floor records, intervention records, action licenses, remainder entries
carrier.maintainer: RAE runtime docs and packet pilot runs

use_episode.use: Fill a packet to orient action in a bounded situation.
use_episode.actor_context: RAE manual pilot or future runtime work
use_episode.inference_drawn: A completed packet can identify licensed actions and blocked consequences for a bounded acting system.
non_inferences: All relevant parties or floors have been captured.
A packet field is equivalent to reality.

binding.binding_target: bounded situation and acting-system response ecology

validity.validity_criterion: Packet fields are filled with evidence, scope, confidence, licensed actions, unlicensed actions, and remainder.
validity.validity_evidence: The packet grammar names the relevant fields and has been used in RAE runs.
validity.uncertainty: No schema validation or cold review is required by the packet itself.

scope.scope_conditions: Bounded situations where a named acting system and horizon are specified.
scope_lost: recognizer standing is not yet explicit in the packet schema
prohibited_scope_extensions: treating packet completion as action success

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing depends on role, affected-party relation, and authority to act in the situation.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: packet readback, decision use, return-capture review

policy_license.action_policy: Packet action-license section plus RAE governance non-laundering rules.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Bounded action guidance when prerequisites, scope, non-claims, and blocked consequences are preserved.
policy_license.license_basis: Completed fields, source evidence, recognizer standing, and governance discipline.

transmission.transmission_path: runtime packet doc -> template -> run packet -> future decision

unlicensed_links: packet schema does not yet require recognizer standing

verdict.next_check: Add schema-backed standing and validation fields before stronger runtime claims.

reflexive_pressure.notes: A packet's licensed action depends on persistent packet grammar, use episode, standing, and action policy together.

symbolic_system.boundary: Reusable record fields for compiling symbolic artifacts into carrier, binding, validity, scope, recognition, consequence, transmission, chain, failure, residue, and verdict structure.

carrier.carrier_state: v0.1 field grammar with recognition and action-policy fields
carrier.carrier_state_space: field names, field definitions, verdict classes, pressure-test examples

use_episode.use: Compile an artifact into structural record fields.
use_episode.actor_context: FSST compiler-seed work and future runtime checker
use_episode.inference_drawn: A symbolic artifact can be made auditable by filling the normal-form fields.
non_inferences: The compiler understands all domain semantics.
A filled record proves the source artifact is valid.
The v0.1 fields are final primitives.

binding.binding_target: structure-preserving representation of symbolic artifact use
binding.operative_mode: prose schema plus pilot record target

validity.validity_criterion: Fields preserve source, carrier, use, validity, scope, standing, policy, transmission, chain, residue, and claim ceiling without overclaim.
validity.validity_evidence: Survived first and adversarial compiler seeds; now has schema pilot.
validity.uncertainty: The compact schema may reveal field pressure not yet reflected in full prose.

scope.scope_conditions: Manual and future semi-structured compilation of symbolic artifacts.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing depends on using the schema within FSST claim ceilings and preserving source evidence.
recognition_authority.authority_source: FSST formation doctrine and compiler seed results
recognition_authority.uptake_path: schema adoption, pilot records, validation checks

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use normal-form records as compiler artifacts.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Treat a valid record as a structural audit object.
policy_license.license_basis: Field conformance, source preservation, standing, and claim ceiling.

transmission.transmission_path: normal-form prose -> JSON schema -> compiled records -> future checker
metadata_lost: some rich prose nuance compressed by compact schema

verdict.next_check: Validate compact schema against project artifacts.

reflexive_pressure.notes: The normal form itself can only license compiler use if object, episode, standing, and policy are all represented.

symbolic_system.boundary: Targets for abstract Lean predicates and watch conditions for future integration.

carrier.carrier_state: predicate lists, theorem targets, checked-slice direction, composition watch condition
carrier.carrier_state_space: type names, predicate signatures, theorem targets, claim ceilings

use_episode.use: Select or constrain next Lean formalization work.
use_episode.inference_drawn: A predicate or theorem target is eligible for Lean work when corpus pressure or prior cases make it load-bearing.
non_inferences: A listed target has already been proved.
Formalization target status grants domain warrant.
The composition watch condition is a current formal target.

binding.binding_target: Lean formalization sequencing and claim ceilings

validity.validity_criterion: Formal targets match discovered structural pressure and preserve non-claims.
validity.validity_evidence: ConsequencePolicy and RecognitionAuthority were selected from corpus pressure and AXLE-checked.
validity.uncertainty: The exact integration theorem is deferred until project artifacts pressure it.

scope.scope_conditions: FSST abstract Lean targets and proof-governance surfaces.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing is split: AXLE recognizes Lean validity; maintainers recognize target relevance.
recognition_authority.authority_source: AXLE for syntax/proof checking; FSST formation docs for target selection.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: formal target doc -> Lean file -> AXLE report -> doctrine list

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Start or defer Lean integration work.
policy_license.action_policy: Corpus-pressure discipline and composition watch condition.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Do isolated or integration Lean work only when artifact pressure warrants it.
policy_license.license_basis: Recognized target relevance plus proof-checking route.

transmission.transmission_path: formation target doc -> Lean implementation -> AXLE result -> future formal core

verdict.next_check: 1C should determine whether integration pressure is strong enough.

reflexive_pressure.notes: This record exposes two recognizers with different standing: AXLE for proof validity and corpus pressure for relevance.

symbolic_system.boundary: Audit prompts for CDP artifacts as maintained symbolic carrier-regimes.

carrier.carrier_state: active candidate meta-governance with no production graph/kernel/formal claim authorization
carrier.carrier_state_space: audit prompts, failure families, required audit packet fields
carrier.maintainer: CDP runtime docs and future review processes

use_episode.use: Audit a CDP artifact before promotion, reuse, or transmission.
use_episode.actor_context: CDP runtime and production graph maintenance
use_episode.inference_drawn: A CDP artifact can be operationally routable while invalid in binding, scope, mode, or correction.
non_inferences: The audit adds a CDP primitive.
The audit authorizes production graph change.

validity.validity_criterion: Audit preserves distinction between operational routability and valid claim binding.
validity.validity_evidence: Artifact explicitly states no graph, kernel, formal, or domain warrant authorization.
validity.confidence: high for claim ceiling; medium for operational uptake
validity.uncertainty: How consistently it is applied before promotion is not shown here.

scope.scope_conditions: Major CDP runtime protocols, admissions, comparison packets, formalization close records, and collection milestones.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing depends on CDP role and promotion gate, not merely audit completion.
recognition_authority.authority_source: CDP runtime governance and operator authorization.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: audit packet -> review/promotion gate -> refusal or admission path

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Use audit result to affect CDP promotion or reuse.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Block, defer, or condition promotion; not admit graph material by itself.
policy_license.license_basis: Audit claim ceiling, recognizer standing, and promotion-gate policy.

transmission.transmission_path: runtime audit doc -> audit packet -> gate decision

verdict.next_check: Require audit packet presence in actual promotion workflows.

reflexive_pressure.notes: This is the clearest example requiring persistent system, use, standing, and consequence policy together.

symbolic_system.boundary: Boundary between runtime-ratified artifacts and canonical production graph admission.

carrier.carrier_state: conditions for graph admission/refusal with required inputs and outputs
carrier.carrier_state_space: required inputs, gate conditions, outputs, scope, future work
carrier.maintainer: CDP runtime docs and promoter implementation

use_episode.use: Evaluate whether a runtime-ratified graph candidate may enter the production graph.
use_episode.inference_drawn: A candidate passing all conditions may be admitted; a failing candidate must receive a refusal record.
non_inferences: Runtime ratification alone admits production graph material.
Operator preference can weaken a failed gate.
A clean schema shape licenses confidence upgrade.

validity.validity_criterion: All listed gate conditions pass and runtime review ceiling is preserved.
validity.validity_evidence: Gate conditions explicitly specify inputs, refusal behavior, confidence ceiling, provenance, and open flags.
validity.confidence: high for rule clarity; unassessed for implementation completeness
validity.uncertainty: Promoter behavior and all linked artifacts are not validated in this record.

scope.scope_conditions: One runtime-ratified graph candidate at a time with explicit operator instruction envelope.

recognition_authority.recognizer_standing: Standing is distributed: schema validates shape, review consumer ratifies runtime status, operator authorizes specific admission, promoter executes.
recognition_authority.authority_source: CDP gate protocol plus operator instruction reference.
recognition_authority.uptake_path: candidate artifacts -> deterministic checks -> admission/refusal output

policy_license.attempted_consequence: Admit candidate to canonical production graph.
policy_license.licensed_consequence: Admission only if every gate condition passes; refusal otherwise.
policy_license.license_basis: Valid inputs, standing recognizers, explicit operator instruction, schema pass, review ceiling preservation.

transmission.transmission_path: runtime ratification -> production gate -> admitted graph edge or refusal record

verdict.next_check: Compiler schema should represent distributed standing and all-or-refuse action policy.

reflexive_pressure.notes: This artifact forces the full chain and shows multiple recognizers with distinct standing roles.
